subject: Add DCAE Microservice Health Check
commit_message:
Add DCAE Microservice Health Check

Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2660
Signed-off-by: Jack Lucas <jflos@sonoris.net>
Change-Id: I0f7488008e7595e493176138b5f449b33ddb0f1b

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

Requires changes in oom/kubernetes/robot/resources/config/eteshare/config/robot_properties.py to set global variables:
GLOBAL_INJECTED_DCAE_MS_IP_ADDR = '{{include "robot.ingress.svchost" (dict "root" . "hostname" "dcae-ms-healthcheck") }}'
# dcae microservice info - everything is from the private oam network (also called onap private network)
GLOBAL_DCAE_MS_SERVER_PROTOCOL = "http"
GLOBAL_DCAE_MS_HEALTH_SERVER_PORT = '{{include "robot.ingress.port" (dict "root" . "hostname" "dcae-healthcheck" "port" 8080) }}'
GLOBAL_DCAE_MS_USERNAME = '{{ .Values.dcaeMsUsername }}'
GLOBAL_DCAE_MS_PASSWORD = '{{ .Values.dcaeMsPassword}}'
GLOBAL_DCAE_AUTHENTICATION = [GLOBAL_DCAE_USERNAME, GLOBAL_DCAE_PASSWORD]

Also change to oom/kubernetes/robot/values.yaml to set dcaeMsUsername and dcaeMsPassword. (The actual values don't matter as long as they're syntactically valid as usename and password, respectively.)

Tested by:
-- Deploying DCAE and a limited set of other ONAP components (including robot) using a locally-modified set of Helm charts that include the changes described above.
-- Creating a modified version of the xtesting-healthcheck image that includes the testsuite updates submitted here.
-- Creating a kubernetes Job that runs the modified xtesting-healthcheck image.
The results were as expected:  DCAE microservices are reported as healthy when all the expected
services are running and ready, and reported as unhealthy when an expected container is not running
and/or ready.

Patch Set 1: Verified+1 Code-Review+2

(1 comment)

note the verification is manual because we exclude the robot linter (too many warning reported - but not linked to this specific patch - there is a linting technical debt with robot)
